# Patterns for agent-generated tests

Agents turn findings into regressable tests. Every generated test lives at
`qa/generated-tests/{flow-id}-{slug}.generated.test.tsx` and follows the
patterns below so humans can review them without re-deriving context.

## Header — always present

```tsx
/**
 * Generated by {agent-name} during flow {flow-id} on {ISO date}.
 * Seed finding: {short sentence}. Severity: {low|medium|high|critical}.
 *
 * This file is regression bait — if it starts failing, the library
 * regressed on behaviour the QA harness previously validated.
 */
```

## Result schema (agent → report)

Every flow run returns:

```json
{
  "flow": "apiresponse-contract",
  "status": "pass" | "fail" | "blocked",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical",
      "kind": "bug" | "edge-case" | "docs-drift" | "dx-papercut",
      "summary": "one sentence",
      "evidence": "testid / console output / network call"
    }
  ],
  "generated_tests": ["qa/generated-tests/apiresponse-contract-invalid-shape.generated.test.tsx"],
  "assertions": [{ "id": "a1", "status": "pass" }],
  "duration_ms": 1234
}
```

Only fill the fields you actually exercised. Blank arrays are fine.

## Vitest patterns to imitate

### Mutation contract test

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
import { renderHook, act } from '@testing-library/react';
import { createWrapper } from '../../src/test/utils/createWrapper';
import { createDomainApi, z } from '../../src';

const schema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(1) });
const api = createDomainApi('todos', schema, schema);

describe('apiresponse-contract: invalid payload', () => {
  it('returns a discriminated error, never throws', async () => {
    const { result } = renderHook(() => api.useCreate(), { wrapper: createWrapper() });
    let res: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof result.current.mutate>> | undefined;
    await act(async () => {
      res = await result.current.mutate({ title: '' });
    });
    expect(res?.success).toBe(false);
    if (!res!.success) {
      expect(res!.error.code).toBe('VALIDATION');
    }
  });
});
```

- `createWrapper()` comes from `src/test/utils/createWrapper.tsx`. If it is
  not suitable, copy the wrapper shape into the test file — do not add a new
  helper to `src/`.
- Never use `try { await mutate() } catch`. The contract is that mutations
  never throw.

## What NOT to generate

- Tests that depend on Playwright or a running sandbox — those belong in
  the flow prompt. `qa/generated-tests/` is pure Vitest.
- Snapshot tests — they rot. Prefer explicit assertions.
- Network-dependent tests — everything must run fully offline.
- Tests that call into `src/test/utils/` files that do not exist.

## Naming

`qa/generated-tests/{flow-id}-{short-slug}.generated.test.tsx`

Examples:
- `apiresponse-contract-nested-field-error.generated.test.tsx`
- `dedup-concurrent-list-hooks.generated.test.tsx`
- `url-stepper-invalid-value-default.generated.test.tsx`
